query: Big Tech M&A activity 2026 pace, record $10B+ deals, and antitrust environment for large tech acquisitions in 2026
answer:
I've completed comprehensive research on Big Tech M&A activity in 2026. Here's a summary of the key findings:

## Summary

**Pace (Record-Setting):** Global M&A hit **~$2.8 trillion in H1 2026** (LSEG), up ~48-50% YoY — the highest first-half total since records began in 1980 — even as deal *count* fell ~9% to a six-year low. Bain projects full-year 2026 to top **$5.3 trillion** (near the 2020 record of $5.6T), potentially the second-highest year ever. Value is concentrated in fewer, bigger deals.

**Record $10B+ Mega-Deals:** This is the defining feature. LSEG counted **47 mega-deals ($10B+) totaling >$1.3 trillion in H1 — nearly 50% of all volumes, an all-time record share.** Bain found $10B+ deals grew 52% in number and 53% in value YoY. Marquee deals include SpaceX/Cursor ($60B, largest AI software deal ever), NextEra/Dominion (~$66.8B, AI-power-driven), and Kone/TK Elevator ($34.4B). Technology was the largest sector at $649B. Notably, Big Tech's dominant capital deployment is AI *capex* (~$650-725B combined), not M&A.

**Antitrust Environment:** Materially **more permissive** under the second Trump administration. FTC (Ferguson) and DOJ (Slater) now favor **negotiated divestitures/remedies over blocking deals** — a sharp break from the Biden era. Enforcement isn't dead (FTC challenged 9 mergers in 2025 vs. 5 previously), but it's remedy-oriented, and advisers say the administration is "receptive to big US combinations." On Big Tech specifically, the Google Search case ended in **no breakup** (data-sharing mandates instead, now under appeal).

The structured output with 14 key facts, 12 sources, and 22 quantitative data points has been recorded. Note the important methodology caveat: LSEG, Bain, and PwC use different windows yielding different full-year projections ($4T PwC vs. $5.3T Bain), and figures are *announced* (not closed) deal values.

sources.snippet: M&A rose 41% YoY to $2.4T in first five months of 2026; on track to top $5.3 trillion; $10B+ deals grew 52% in number and 53% in value.

sources.title: One year into Trump 2.0: enforcement agenda of US antitrust agencies
sources.snippet: Transition to second Trump administration ushered in a much more favorable antitrust enforcement climate for M&A; divestitures and remedies back on the table.

sources.title: Antitrust M&A Outlook for 2026
sources.snippet: FTC challenged 9 mergers in 2025 vs 5 in Biden's final year; DOJ challenged 4 vs 1; early termination resumed (48 times in first 12 days of December).

sources.title: SpaceX to acquire the AI coding startup Cursor for $60 billion
sources.snippet: SpaceX announced acquisition of Anysphere (Cursor) for $60 billion all-stock, June 16, 2026.

sources.title: SpaceX to acquire Cursor for $60B in stock, days after blockbuster IPO
sources.snippet: $60 billion stock deal after SpaceX's historic IPO; largest ever acquisition of a venture-backed startup.

sources.title: Global M&A deal value on track to reach $4 trillion this year: PwC
sources.snippet: PwC: 2026 on track toward ~$4T; $5B+ deals contributed almost half of total value; expects 40% YoY rise in megadeal value. '2026 is the year M&A supersized.'

sources.title: Technology: US Deals 2026 outlook: M&A Trends
sources.snippet: Deal activity forming a barbell: transformational megadeals and small speculative AI tuck-ins, limited mid-market activity.

sources.title: Big Tech AI Capex 2026: Microsoft, Google, Meta & Amazon's ~$725B Spending Race
sources.snippet: Combined 2026 capex guidance ~$725B (Amazon ~$200B, Microsoft ~$190B, Alphabet $175-185B, Meta $115-135B).

sources.title: Big Tech Antitrust Roundup: Google Search - No Breakup
sources.snippet: Sept 2, 2025 Judge Amit Mehta remedies ruling in DOJ v Google found monopolization but ordered no breakup.

sources.title: Google Appeals Antitrust Ruling, Seeks To Lift Data-Sharing Mandate
sources.snippet: Google appealing finding that it monopolized search; asked Judge Mehta to stay data-sharing orders citing irreparable harm.

key_facts: Global announced M&A reached ~$2.8 trillion in H1 2026 (LSEG), up ~48-50% YoY — the highest first-half total since records began in 1980.
H1 2026 deal count fell ~9% to ~24,000, a six-year low, confirming value concentration in fewer, larger deals.
LSEG counted 47 mega-deals of $10B+ in H1 2026 totaling more than $1.3 trillion — nearly 50% of global volumes, an all-time record share.
Bain reports $10B+ deals grew 52% in number and 53% in value YoY in early 2026; global M&A on track to top $5.3 trillion for full-year 2026 (near the 2020 record of $5.6T).
2025 global M&A totaled $4.9 trillion (second-highest annual total on record at that time).
SpaceX agreed to acquire AI coding startup Cursor (Anysphere) for $60 billion all-stock on June 16, 2026 — the largest AI software deal in history, expected to close Q3 2026.
NextEra Energy/Dominion Energy merger (~$66.8B, cited up to $119B) is driven partly by AI data-center power demand.
Technology remained the largest M&A sector globally with $649 billion of announced transactions in H1 2026.
Big Tech (Amazon, Microsoft, Alphabet, Meta) is guiding to ~$650-725 billion combined AI infrastructure capex in 2026, roughly doubling 2025 — dwarfing pure M&A.
The second Trump administration's FTC and DOJ favor negotiated remedies (divestitures/behavioral commitments) over blocking deals, a materially more permissive climate for large tech M&A.
Trump FTC challenged 9 mergers in 2025 (vs 5 in Biden's final year); DOJ challenged 4 (vs 1) — enforcement is remedy-oriented, not dormant.
Judge Amit Mehta's Sept 2025 Google Search remedies ruling ordered no breakup (data-sharing mandates instead); Google appealed Jan 16, 2026 and the US government cross-appealed for stronger remedies.
Cross-border M&A hit $893 billion in H1 2026 (up 62% YoY), best start since 2018; US was the most-targeted country at 25% of cross-border deals.
PwC (different methodology) projects 2026 full-year global deal value toward ~$4 trillion, strongest since 2021, with $5B+ deals contributing almost half of total value.
